principles: Preserve course learning order at module and topic level.
Merge closely related lectures when they form one mental model.
Keep large or independently searchable concepts visible.
Integrate summaries and recaps into parent topics.
Separate reusable concepts from project-specific implementation.
Use supporting transcript lectures when a selected summary or overview is not self-contained.

phase_5_decisions.decision: Keep exactly three primary tracks: JavaScript, React, and Next.js.
phase_5_decisions.reason: This matches the product navigation and the intended prerequisite flow.

phase_5_decisions.decision: Orientation content remains visible but is not scored and does not count toward mastery.
phase_5_decisions.reason: It supports onboarding without distorting learning progress.

phase_5_decisions.decision: Summary and recap lectures are absorbed into module reviews instead of appearing as duplicate lessons.
phase_5_decisions.reason: The platform should use summaries for retrieval practice and consolidation.

phase_5_decisions.decision: Projects are treated as applied case studies or milestone challenges; transferable concepts stay in core lessons.
phase_5_decisions.reason: Learners should understand the concept independently of a course-specific project.

phase_5_decisions.decision: Tailwind CSS and Supabase stay inside the React track as optional ecosystem supplements.
phase_5_decisions.reason: They are relevant to the source course but are not React language fundamentals.

phase_5_decisions.decision: Class components remain optional legacy context.
phase_5_decisions.reason: They are useful for reading older code but are not part of the default modern path.

phase_5_decisions.decision: Supporting implementation lectures may enrich an approved topic without becoming extra top-level topics.
phase_5_decisions.reason: This preserves a clean curriculum while making each lesson standalone and practical.

phase_5_decisions.decision: Assessments are designed now at the objective and format level; full question writing happens alongside lesson production.
phase_5_decisions.reason: Assessment must shape instruction, but exact questions must match the final explanations and examples.

pilot.title: Data Structures and Functional Techniques
pilot.reason: It contains conceptual explanation, code reasoning, API selection, closures, and practical data transformation, making it a representative test of the platform format.
